title: Ordering Groups
excerpt: I use groups to contain related sets of projects. I work in auto, so the groups are by vehicle manufacturer. It is really wild to me that there’s no way to order groups based on progress. Does anybody have a work-around …

posts.author: markmarkmark (Mark)
posts.cookedHtml: I use groups to contain related sets of projects. I work in auto, so the groups are by vehicle manufacturer. It is really wild to me that there’s no way to order groups based on progress. Does anybody have a work-around they use to track which groups need the most work done and which groups are good to go?

posts.author: timlittletech (Tim Little)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @markmarkmark Welcome to the monday community!

it is unfortunate that the group functionality is somewhat limited. However, I think you can accomplish what you are looking for with a ‘chart’ view. Either as a view of the board or as a separate dashboard. Create a Chart and set the x axis to ‘group’
You could stack on a status column to understand which items are complete in each group as well.

Screen Shot 2021-07-24 at 3.15.05 PM2894×1726 272 KB

Hope that helps!
